explanation: This property enforces that after start-up has been completed successfully (startUpDone becomes true), the next user actions must be either selecting "New Patient" (newPatient) or "Resume Ventilation" (resumeVentilation) before the system can transition to SelfTest Mode (SelfTestMode). This matches the requirement by ensuring that whenever SelfTestMode becomes active, it must be the case that (newPatient or resumeVentilation) has happened since the completion of start-up. The property uses a globally quantified implication to ensure that SelfTestMode can only be entered if the user has performed one of the required selections after start-up.

This formula expresses the requirement that globally (at all times), if the system is no longer in startup mode (i.e., startup has completed: startUpDone is true), then before the system transitions to self-test mode (SelfTestMode), the user must select 'New Patient' (newPatient) or 'Resume Ventilation' (resumeVentilation). This is encoded as: whenever SelfTestMode becomes active, it is only allowed if, in the previous state, startUpDone was true, and one of the required user actions (newPatient or resumeVentilation) was taken. The chosen template monitors transitions into SelfTestMode and enforces the correct preconditions, as described in the natural language.

The requirement is: Once start-up has been completed (startUpDone), before transitioning to self-test mode (SelfTestMode), the user must select either 'New Patient' (newPatient) or 'Resume Ventilation' (resumeVentilation). This is a classic 'before' property: SelfTestMode cannot occur unless (newPatient or resumeVentilation) happened after startUpDone and before SelfTestMode. The best way to express this using the given atomic propositions is: it is always the case (globally) that whenever the system is in SelfTestMode, (newPatient or resumeVentilation) must also have previously occurred after startUpDone and before SelfTestMode. However, with the available templates, a common encoding is: it is always the case that if we are in SelfTestMode, then newPatient or resumeVentilation holds (either persists, or must be chosen before that transition).

The requirement specifies that after the start-up process is fully completed (indicated by 'startUpDone'), the user must select either 'New Patient' (newPatient) or 'Resume Ventilation' (resumeVentilation) before the system is allowed to transition into the SelfTest mode (SelfTestMode). This is a classic case of enforcing a precondition: if the system moves into SelfTestMode, it must be the case that either 'newPatient' or 'resumeVentilation' has happened since start up completed. The best way to encode this is using a globally quantified implication: globally, if the system is in SelfTestMode, then either 'newPatient' or 'resumeVentilation' was selected after 'startUpDone'. To simplify, we can enforce that globally, entering SelfTestMode is only allowed if (newPatient | resumeVentilation) is true. Therefore, we use the template 'G (bool_exp2 -> bool_exp3)' with bool_exp2 = SelfTestMode (system in self-test mode) and bool_exp3 = (newPatient | resumeVentilation) (one of these must have occurred before SelfTestMode can be entered). We choose N_DURATION = 1 for this property (duration not relevant for this implication).

The property specifies that whenever start-up has been completed successfully (startUpDone), then it is always the case that the system must only transition to SelfTestMode after the user has chosen either 'New Patient' or 'Resume Ventilation' (i.e., (newPatient | resumeVentilation) becomes true before SelfTestMode can become true). This is captured by the global implication: whenever startUpDone is true, if SelfTestMode occurs, then either newPatient or resumeVentilation must have occurred prior. In the LTL context, this constraint is most directly modeled as: whenever startUpDone holds, always, SelfTestMode implies that newPatient or resumeVentilation has happened before. Since LTL does not support explicit 'before' easily in the simplest forms, a common and expressive approximation is to require that globally, whenever the system is in SelfTestMode, it must be the case that newPatient or resumeVentilation was already selected (i.e., (newPatient | resumeVentilation) is true before or at the same time as SelfTestMode). We choose a template that expresses this requirement: globally, if SelfTestMode is true, then newPatient or resumeVentilation must be true. Since the prompt says 'before the system transitions to self-test mode', this safety property is best captured by template 127: 'Globally, if not SelfTestMode, then (newPatient OR resumeVentilation)' must be true before SelfTestMode can hold.

The requirement is that after successful start-up (startUpDone), before the ventilator enters SelfTest mode (SelfTestMode), the user must select either "New Patient" (newPatient) or "Resume Ventilation" (resumeVentilation). This is best modeled by globally (G), whenever the system is not yet in SelfTestMode but startUpDone has occurred, then either newPatient or resumeVentilation must happen before SelfTestMode occurs. By using the template that enforces, on each transition to SelfTestMode, the condition (newPatient | resumeVentilation) held immediately before, we ensure that it is not possible to enter SelfTestMode unless one of the required selections was made immediately previously. This matches the intent of the requirement, ensuring the user's choice precedes any self-test transition post start-up.

The requirement is that after start-up completion (represented by 'startUpDone'), the system cannot enter self-test mode ('SelfTestMode') until the user has selected either 'New Patient' or 'Resume Ventilation' (represented by 'newPatient' or 'resumeVentilation'). The chosen template enforces that globally, if start-up is done and self-test mode is entered, then, either 'New Patient' or 'Resume Ventilation' must have been selected. The choice of template 40 (G ((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> bool_exp3)) fits as it checks if both startUpDone and SelfTestMode are true (system transitions to self-test mode after start-up), then one of the user options must be chosen.

The requirement is that, after start-up is completed (indicated by 'startUpDone'), before the system can enter self-test mode ('SelfTestMode'), the user must make a selection: either 'New Patient' or 'Resume Ventilation' (indicated by 'newPatient' or 'resumeVentilation'). This means that whenever the system transitions into 'SelfTestMode', it must have already seen 'startUpDone', and at least one of 'newPatient' or 'resumeVentilation' must have occurred between. The temporal logical pattern here is best captured by a response property: Whenever the system enters 'SelfTestMode', it must be the case that 'startUpDone' occurred in the past and that, after it, either 'newPatient' or 'resumeVentilation' occurred before or at the same time as 'SelfTestMode'. The LTL property 'G (SelfTestMode -> ((newPatient | resumeVentilation) & startUpDone))' ensures that whenever SelfTestMode becomes true, both startUpDone and (newPatient or resumeVentilation) are true at that point, which conservatively enforces the requirement. I use N_DURATION = 1 as the minimal time window for checking occurrence just before or at transition (can increase if checking strictly 'before').

The requirement states: 'Once the start-up has been completed successfully the user must select “New Patient” or “Resume Ventilation” before the system transitions to self-test mode.' To formalize this, we use the atomic propositions 'startUpDone' for start-up completion, 'newPatient' and 'resumeVentilation' to represent the two required user actions, and 'SelfTestMode' for system transition to self-test mode. 

The property must ensure: if startUpDone becomes true, then SelfTestMode cannot be true before either newPatient or resumeVentilation is selected. This means that as long as startUpDone is true and neither newPatient nor resumeVentilation has occurred, SelfTestMode must not be allowed. The most direct template for this requirement is:

G ( (startUpDone & (! (newPatient | resumeVentilation))) -> (! SelfTestMode))

This states: 'Globally, if startUpDone is true and neither newPatient nor resumeVentilation is true, then SelfTestMode must not be true.'

This directly encodes the input requirement that SelfTestMode cannot occur after start-up unless one of 'newPatient' or 'resumeVentilation' is selected. When either newPatient or resumeVentilation becomes true, the constraint is lifted and SelfTestMode can follow.

The property requires that once the start-up has been completed successfully (represented by 'startUpDone'), the system must not transition to SelfTest Mode ('SelfTestMode') until after the user has selected either 'New Patient' or 'Resume Ventilation'. This is enforced as follows: whenever 'startUpDone' occurs, it must be the case that 'SelfTestMode' cannot become true until at least one of 'newPatient' or 'resumeVentilation' have occurred. The chosen template expresses that after 'startUpDone', 'SelfTestMode' is only allowed if 'newPatient' or 'resumeVentilation' are true, matching the requirement specified in natural language. N_DURATION is set to 5 as a reasonable fixed example duration value for the property.
